authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Tamarack Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Tamarack Lake not listed)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Lake layer record for gaz_id 268080, "Tamarack Lake", Reservoir, Alpine County, CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":268080,"gaz_name":"Tamarack Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Reservoir","fcode":39004,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Alpine"}; geometry (Web Mercator -13347293.7919, 4666149.9688) converts to lon -119.900780, lat 38.611331, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-119.9008, 38.6113) to within ~3m.

sources.title: Tamarack Lake Topo Map, Alpine County, California (Pacific Valley quad)
Blue Lakes — Carson Pass
sources.quotedText: A mile before Blue Lakes Road arrives at Lower Blue Lake is a turnoff to Tamarack Lake (shown right with the Nipple in the background) and the Indian Valley area. The trail winds for 3 miles past a series of shallow lakes - Tamarack, Sunset, and Wet Meadows.

parameters.note: Tamarack Lake is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake/reservoir regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake/reservoir regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license
